title: poa nemoralis subsp. juncoides
intro: Poa nemoralis subsp. juncoides, also known as red fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of red fescue include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and aiding in the removal of mucus from the lungs. Red fescue can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in herbal remedies for respiratory issues, as well as for its calming effects on the nervous system.
